top_entities.name: Ukraine
top_entities.news_summary: JULY CIVILIAN TOLL HIGHEST IN 3 YEARS (within window): July 2026 civilian toll: 286 killed and 1,388 injured — highest monthly rate since 2023. Includes massive July 1-2 Kyiv attack: 70+ missiles (28 ballistic), ~500 drones; 27+ killed, 100+ injured; 9-floor residential building collapsed Darnytskyi district. 52,500 sheltered in Kyiv metro. European Parliament adopted resolution demanding accountability. Special Tribunal for Russia crime of aggression now active. 62,716 civilian casualties since Feb 2022. 3.7M internally displaced; 9.6M Ukrainians displaced overall.

top_entities.name: Venezuela
top_entities.news_summary: EARTHQUAKE DAY 9 (July 3): Death toll 2,595 officially; forensic pathologist tells CNN official figure is "not even a third of what is actually there" — morgue processing ~400 bodies/day in La Guaira alone. 50,000 unaccounted; 12,400+ injured; 60,000 buildings damaged/destroyed. 862+ aftershocks. Water system failed in some areas. 20+ country rescue teams. Government coordination failures persist. US commitment $300M.

top_entities.name: China
top_entities.news_summary: ETHNIC UNITY LAW IN FORCE + TRANSNATIONAL ENFORCEMENT (July 1, 2026): Law came into effect July 1. Article 63 explicitly applies to organizations and individuals OUTSIDE China — diaspora Uyghur, Tibetan, Mongolian communities globally now legally liable under Chinese law. Amnesty International condemns as "legal blueprint for cultural erasure." Germany: Foreign Ministry expresses "great concern" about transnational repression risk. Law compels assimilation by 56 ethnic groups; defines ethnic division terms broadly for arbitrary enforcement. China Coast Guard patrols Taiwan eastern EEZ continuing.

top_entities.name: Lebanon
top_entities.news_summary: CEASEFIRE FRAGILITY — IDF "NO CEASEFIRE" IN SOUTH (ongoing within window): IDF chief says "no ceasefire" in south Lebanon amid continuing fighting with Hezbollah. April 16 ceasefire framework has seen continuous violations. 4,000+ killed since Mar 2 resumption; 1M+ displaced (>20% of population). 76% of south Lebanon farmers displaced; 22% of agricultural land damaged. More than 1M facing food insecurity crisis months ahead. Lebanon-Hezbollah peace terms still at impasse: Hezbollah demands full Israeli withdrawal; US framework ties it to Hezbollah disarmament. Doha talks include Lebanon stabilization.

top_entities.name: Nigeria
top_entities.news_summary: LEAN SEASON PEAK — UPDATED JULY 2026 DATA (July 2, 2026): WFP July 2026 report: 36.2M food insecure in Nigeria — highest ever recorded. 15,000 at IPC Phase 5 Catastrophe in Borno State. WFP able to support only 740,000 of 6.2M in need in northeast — 5.5M children and families without lifesaving food aid. Renewed JNIM cross-border attacks threatening regional stability. June-August lean season at peak. FAO-WFP highest-concern global hotspot.

top_entities.name: South Sudan
top_entities.news_summary: FAMINE RISK — IPC PHASE 5 IN 4 COUNTIES (July 2026 data): 7.8M people at high levels of acute food insecurity (IPC Phase 3+). 2.2M children acutely malnourished. 73,300 at IPC Phase 5 Catastrophe — 160% increase from prior estimate. Credible risk of famine in four counties in Upper Nile and Jonglei states. 300,000 displaced in Jonglei alone. 200,000 displaced from Akobo County since March 6. Cholera, malaria, measles outbreaks compounding malnutrition.

top_entities.name: Mali
top_entities.news_summary: JNIM BAMAKO SIEGE ONGOING + LEAN SEASON PEAK (within window): JNIM siege of Bamako continues — blocking all major supply roads from Ivory Coast, Senegal, Mauritania; severe shortages of fuel, food, and basic goods for weeks. 5M in need of humanitarian aid. 1.56M acutely food insecure; pockets of famine-like conditions in north. April 25 coordinated attacks on Bamako, Gao, Kati, Kidal — largest offensive since 2012. Humanitarian response 18.5% funded. Contested whether JNIM has fully surrounded Bamako — ongoing tactical evolution.

top_entities.name: UnitedHealth Group
top_entities.news_summary: DOJ CRIMINAL + CIVIL PROBE CONTINUING (July 2026): DOJ criminal probe extended to Optum Rx and physician reimbursement arrangements. Civil probe: inflated diagnoses to trigger higher Medicare Advantage payments. Third-party review ongoing. Senate Grassley investigation: UnitedHealth "aggressively gaming" Medicare Advantage. Q2 earnings call July 29. DOJ June 23 national healthcare fraud takedown ($6.5B, 455 defendants) maintains sector-wide regulatory pressure. CMS suspended 1,079 providers.

top_entities.name: El Salvador
top_entities.news_summary: CONSTITUTIONAL AMENDMENT + CRISTOSAL SHUTDOWN (July 2026): Legislative Assembly amended constitution allowing indefinite presidential re-election. Cristosal — leading human rights org — suspended operations, citing escalating repression (choice between prison and exile). 86 political prisoners documented; 245+ facing political persecution since Bukele took power. Life prison sentences for people as young as 12 signed into law April 2026. State of exception extends into Year 5. Mass arbitrary detention, enforced disappearance, torture, due process violations ongoing.

top_entities.name: Sudan
top_entities.news_summary: RSF EL OBEID OFFENSIVE + AMNESTY CRIMES AGAINST HUMANITY (ongoing within window): RSF escalating drone strikes on El Obeid (capital North Kordofan); 563,000 residents and 105,000 IDPs at risk. Daily drone strikes on aid trucks; fuel/water running out within weeks. UN High Commissioner Turk: "Stop this madness." UK warns of siege conditions. 29+ countries raised alarm. Separately, Amnesty International released July 2026 report confirming RSF "crimes against humanity and ethnic cleansing" at El Fasher (May 2024–Oct 2025). 28.9M food insecure. More people in famine in Sudan than rest of world combined.

top_entities.name: Iran
top_entities.news_summary: KHAMENEI STATE FUNERAL BEGINS (July 3–9): Casket of assassinated Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei (killed Feb 28 in Israeli strikes) placed on display today July 3. Week-long funeral processions July 4–9 (Tehran, Qom, Najaf/Karbala, Mashhad). US-Iran nuclear talks EXPLICITLY PAUSED during funeral period. Next round to be scheduled after July 9. Talks had been making progress on Hormuz and Lebanon issues. Son Mojtaba (wounded in same strikes) will not attend over security concerns. 100+ country representatives attending. Iran composite 2.5 — critical band.

top_entities.name: Democratic Republic of C
top_entities.news_summary: EBOLA OUTBREAK — 4 PROVINCES + INTERNATIONAL SPREAD (as of July 2, 2026): 1,406 confirmed cases; 438 deaths. Bundibugyo strain — no approved vaccine or treatment. Ituri (primary, 1,283 cases/366 deaths), North Kivu, South Kivu, and Haut-Uele (4th province, bordering South Sudan/CAR, confirmed June 29). Uganda: cases in Kampala. France: imported case confirmed. Germany: US citizen medically evacuated for treatment. International community mobilizing but surveillance gaps remain significant.

top_entities.name: Burkina Faso
top_entities.news_summary: LEAN SEASON PEAK + JNIM BLOCKADES (June-August 2026 peak confirmed): 9.1M food insecure across AES states (Burkina Faso, Mali, Niger) during June-August 2026 lean season. JNIM siege blockades affecting ~2M people across 40+ localities (Centre-Nord, Est, North, Sahel regions). 4.5M displaced including 2.9M children. Humanitarian response severely access-constrained. WFP helicopter deliveries only route for many communities. Junta continues to restrict independent reporting.

top_entities.name: Russia
top_entities.news_summary: KYIV ATTACK ACCOUNTABILITY + HIGHEST JULY CIVILIAN TOLL IN 3 YEARS (within window): July 2026: 286 Ukrainians killed + 1,388 injured — highest monthly civilian toll since 2023. July 1-2 Kyiv attack: 70+ missiles, ~500 drones; 27+ killed; residential building collapsed. EU sanctioned 9 Bucha massacre perpetrators March 2026. Special Tribunal for crime of aggression against Ukraine now operational (EC joined May 2026). EU sanctions renewed until July 2027. 20,500+ Ukrainian children forcibly deported to Russia. 200,000+ international crimes under investigation.

top_entities.name: Yemen
top_entities.news_summary: HUMANITARIAN SYSTEM STRESS — 73 UN STAFF DETAINED BY HOUTHIS (ongoing within window): 18.3M people acutely food insecure; further deterioration projected. 73 UN staff detained by Houthi de facto authorities — severely restricting aid delivery across areas home to ~70% of humanitarian needs. 450+ health facilities closed. Health system collapsing. US airstrikes on Houthi infrastructure continuing (within Iran war context). Houthi cross-Red Sea shipping attacks: 14 seafarer deaths from IMO. IPC: tens of thousands risk catastrophic hunger/famine-like conditions.

sector_alerts.summary: July 3, 2026 (Day 9): Official death toll 2,595. CNN July 2 report: forensic pathologist at makeshift La Guaira morgue processing ~400 bodies per day says "the government death toll is not even a third of what is actually there." ~50,000 still unaccounted. 60,000 buildings damaged/destroyed. 12,400+ injured. sector_alerts.title: DRC Ebola Bundibugyo: 4th province confirmed, international cases in Uganda/France/Germany — no approved vaccine
sector_alerts.summary: As of July 2, 2026: 1,406 confirmed DRC cases; 438 deaths. Bundibugyo strain — no approved vaccine or curative treatment. Fourth province Haut-Uele (bordering South Sudan and CAR) confirmed June 29 — raises cross-border spillover risk. Uganda: all cases in Kampala (capital city — international hub risk). France: one imported case. Germany: US citizen medically evacuated for treatment. ECDC considers EU/EEA infection likelihood "very low" but notes significant surveillance gaps. UNHCR seeking $14M for regional preparedness.

sector_alerts.title: Sudan RSF El Obeid: UN warns "impending atrocities"; Amnesty confirms El Fasher crimes against humanity
sector_alerts.summary: July 2026: RSF escalating drone strikes on El Obeid (North Kordofan capital) killing civilians, cutting fuel/food/water access to 563,000 residents + 105,000 IDPs. 29+ countries raised alarm. IRC: "imminent humanitarian catastrophe mirroring El Fasher." UN Rights Chief Turk: "Stop this madness." UK warns of siege conditions. Separately, Amnesty International published July 2026 report documenting RSF "crimes against humanity and ethnic cleansing" at El Fasher from May 2024–October 2025. Sudan has more people in famine than rest of world combined.

sector_alerts.title: China Ethnic Unity Law active July 1: transnational reach — diaspora communities globally now legally liable
sector_alerts.summary: July 1, 2026: China's Ethnic Unity and Progress Promotion Law came into force. Article 63 explicitly holds individuals and organizations OUTSIDE China legally liable for acts deemed to "undermine ethnic unity." Amnesty International: law "entrenches assimilation of minority groups" — primarily targeting Uyghurs, Tibetans, Mongolians. Germany Foreign Ministry: "great concern" about transnational repression risk to diaspora communities in Europe. Center for Uyghur Studies: "legal blueprint for cultural erasure." Law arrives alongside China Coast Guard patrols of Taiwan EEZ continuing.

sector_alerts.title: US Supreme Court June 30: birthright citizenship upheld; 4th Appeals Court rejects mandatory detention
sector_alerts.summary: June 30, 2026 (within window): Supreme Court upheld the Fourteenth Amendment guarantee of birthright citizenship — rejected Trump executive order ending citizenship for children born to undocumented parents. Same day: Tenth Circuit ruled mandatory immigration detention without bond hearings unlawful — fourth federal appeals court to reach this conclusion. These represent significant civil rights wins amid broad administration crackdowns. Context: Columbia University agreed to $221M payment to restore $1.3B in frozen federal funding, with enhanced foreign student surveillance requirements.

entity_reviews.summary: FAMINE RISK — IPC PHASE 5 IN 4 COUNTIES (July 2026 data): 7.8M people at high levels of acute food insecurity (IPC Phase 3+). 2.2M children acutely malnourished. 73,300 at IPC Phase 5 Catastrophe — 160% increase from prior estimate. Cholera, malaria, measles outbreaks compounding malnutrition.

entity_reviews.summary: HUMANITARIAN CRISIS — 8.25M IN NEED (within window): 8.25M people require humanitarian assistance. 6.5M at IPC Phase 3+ (February-March 2026); 2M+ at Emergency (Phase 4). 3.8M displaced. 1.85M children facing acute malnutrition. Al-Shabaab attacks on civilian infrastructure and cross-clan clashes ongoing. June 3 clashes: 13 killed, 189 wounded; ceasefire partially holding. Drought affecting 4.6M. Significant funding shortfalls — agencies scaling back.
